items.title: Is it time to buy IT ? Fund managers suggest gradual addition by investors
items.summary: Indian IT stocks are at their cheapest since mid-2020, presenting a buying opportunity. Fund managers suggest gradual accumulation over two years as valuations have significantly compressed. Despite concerns over AI and recent sell-offs, the sector's improving deal momentum and expanding market offer potential for value investors. A balanced approach combining lump sum and staggered investments is advised.

items.title: No need for a rate hike unless inflation spikes: Economists
items.summary: Bank economists have told the Reserve Bank of India that there is no immediate need to raise the repo rate or change the monetary policy stance. This advice is based on inflation remaining within the central bank's tolerance band. The RBI has alternative tools to manage currency pressures.

items.title: Oil Price Today (April 2): Oil jumps 5% to cross $106/barrel after Trump's comments erase de-escalation hopes
items.summary: Oil prices have jumped back above $106 a barrel. President Donald Trump's speech revived fears of more conflict in the Middle East. This comes after earlier hopes for peace had briefly lowered prices. Experts believe oil prices will stay high due to damage and risks. The situation suggests continued volatility in the short term.

items.title: ETMarkets Smart Talk | FII comeback will be key trigger for next rally in Indian markets: Saibal Ghosh
items.summary: Foreign investor inflows are expected to fuel the next market surge. India's long-term growth story remains strong despite recent volatility. A cooling of global AI hype could draw foreign funds back. Investors should focus on domestic growth, particularly banking and financial services. Gold offers a hedge against uncertainty, with structural allocation advised long-term.

items.title: Bulls take stage as markets hope it's curtains on war
items.summary: Indian stock markets saw a significant rebound on Wednesday. Hopes for peace in West Asia and falling oil prices boosted investor sentiment. The NSE Nifty and BSE Sensex climbed substantially. While markets experienced a strong rally, some gains were pared by the close. Investors remain cautious as geopolitical signals shift. Sectoral indices across the board closed higher.

items.title: Buy selectively, focus on resilient sectors despite volatility: Manish Sonthalia
items.summary: Indian markets face geopolitical and inflationary headwinds, with a prolonged economic adjustment expected until FY28. Domestic investors find opportunities in corrected valuations, focusing on resilient stocks and sectors like commodities, defence, and healthcare. Selectivity is key, particularly within financials, as the market navigates short-term turbulence.

items.title: Advice Line with Angie & Dan Bastian of Angie's BOOMCHICKAPOP
items.summary: Today’s callers: Michelle from California assesses the trade offs of accepting outside investment to scale her organic granola brand. Then, Gloria from Connecticut wonders how to overcome stigma and get more people talking about her pelvic floor therapy device. And Eric from Australia evaluates new markets for his maple-based sports nutrition products.  Plus, Dan and Angie’s take on why even the busiest entrepreneur should find time to turn off their phone at the dinner table...  Thank
items.source_name: How I Built This with Guy Raz

items.title: Elizabeth Warren’s Bold Plan To Tax The Ultra-Wealthy Sparks Debate
items.summary: Senator Elizabeth Warren’s Ultra-Millionaire Tax would levy 2–3% on the ultra-wealthy, raising trillions while sparking debate over legality and fairness.

items.title: Rhys Hoskins Responds To Phillies Challenge After Bryce Harper Reunion Report
items.summary: The former Philadelphia Phillies slugger sent a reminder about his own place in the team’s history after news of a potential return turned heads.

items.title: Acclaimed Physicist And His Daughter Are Burying Tiny Nuclear Reactors A Mile Underground
items.summary: Liz Muller convinced her dad Richard to forego retirement and become an entrepreneur. The result is a revolutionary approach to making atomic energy cheaper and safer.

items.title: U.S. Embassy Warns Iran-Linked Militias May Attack Baghdad In Next 24-48 Hours And Target Americans
items.summary: The Embassy urged citizens to leave Iraq and warned the attacks may target both American people and entities like U.S. businesses, universities and diplomatic facilities.

items.title: Why U.S. Gatling Guns Are Not Stopping Iran’s Shahed Drones
items.summary: Gatling-type weapons like the U.S. C-RAM can be presented as an invincible shield against drones. But some Shaheds are getting through due to the weapon's limitations.

items.title: USMNT Has Hardest World Cup Group Of Pot 1 Teams, Per FIFA Rankings
items.summary: The Americans may have avoided world superpowers, but they're also in the only 2026 World Cup group where every team is ranked in the global top 40.

items.title: New York Restaurants April 2026: Where To Go
items.summary: Edible enlightenment from our eatery experts and colleagues Monie Begley, Richard Nalley and Randall Lane, as well as brothers Bob, Kip and Tim.

items.title: Dodgers World Series Champ Sends Blue Jays Message After Game 7 Confrontation
items.summary: A Los Angeles Dodgers champion offered a response to Toronto Blue Jays fans after initiating a bench-clearing showdown in the World Series.
